attributes.readMe:
## Popover

An anchored floating surface that hosts a focused interaction next to a source element ‚Äî it **raises** a focused something out of the source's small footprint without taking the user away from the source. A standalone, remixable extraction of the `boxel-surface` `Lift` primitive whose only npm dependency is `@floating-ui/dom` (shimmed as a global virtual module in every realm).

### Orthogonal axes
Each dial does exactly one thing and reads only its own value, so any combination is predictable:
- **kind** ‚Äî `details` ¬∑ `edit` ¬∑ `tools` ‚Äî ARIA role + autofocus default + CSS theme
- **anchoring** ‚Äî `beside` ¬∑ `overlay` ¬∑ `center` ‚Äî position only
- **size** ‚Äî `compact` ¬∑ `comfortable` ¬∑ `spacious` ¬∑ `auto` ‚Äî min/max width + height
- **backdrop** ‚Äî `none` ¬∑ `tint` ¬∑ `blur` ¬∑ `dim` ‚Äî surface material
- **elevation** ‚Äî `flat` ¬∑ `raised` ¬∑ `elevated` ¬∑ `floating` ‚Äî shadow depth + radius

### Keyboard / focus (explicit opt-ins)
- **@keyboardModel** ‚Äî `'pick'` (focus a `[role=listbox]`, route arrow keys) or `'edit'` (focus the editor input, route typing keys). Omit to leave key handling to the host.
- **@trapFocus** ‚Äî trap Tab inside the popover (aria-modal). Use for editor popovers that own the full focus cycle.

### Escalation
Pass `@canEscalateTo` (kinds other than the current `@kind`) plus `@onEscalate` to render a compact corner glyph (‚úé ‚Üí edit, ‚ìò ‚Üí details, ‚ãØ ‚Üí tools / multiple targets). The toolbar is OFF by default; single-kind contracts get no chrome.

### Per-kind content via named blocks
The host supplies one named block per kind ‚Äî `<:details>`, `<:edit>`, `<:tools>` ‚Äî and the popover renders the block matching the current `@kind`. No host-side `{{#if (eq kind ‚Ä¶)}}` dispatch: changing `@kind` (including via escalation) swaps the rendered block live while the popover stays open. Supply a block for every kind reachable through `@kind` or `@canEscalateTo` (a missing block renders an empty pane); a single-kind popover writes just one block. Each block's content is wrapped in `.bx-popover__pane` with `data-bx-popover-pane='<kind>'` ‚Äî a styling hook with **no padding** (the body's picker convention applies: each editor pane owns its own gutter).

### Calling convention
The host owns **what** (open/close state, kind state, body content); the Popover owns **how** (positioning, dismissal on Esc / outside-click, focus enter/restore, per-kind chrome, the optional dim overlay).

## Usage

The **Popover Playground** example is the canonical reference ‚Äî every axis is a live control there, and it prints the exact invocation as you change dials. The minimal shape:

```gts
import Popover from '../popover.gts';

<template>
  <button data-anchor='preview'>Open</button>

  <Popover
    @anchor='[data-anchor=preview]'
    @open={{this.open}}
    @kind='details'
    @anchoring='beside'
    @placement='bottom'
    @offset={{8}}
    @size='comfortable'
    @backdrop='none'
    @elevation='raised'
    @onDismiss={{this.close}}
  >
    <:details>
      ‚Ä¶details content‚Ä¶
    </:details>
  </Popover>
</template>
```

- `@anchor` is a CSS selector string the popover positions against.
- `@open` + `@onDismiss` ‚Äî the host owns open/close state; the popover calls `@onDismiss` on Esc / outside-click.
- `@placement` / `@offset` / `@arrow` apply only when `@anchoring='beside'`.
- For an editor popover, add `@kind='edit'`, `@keyboardModel='edit'`, and `@trapFocus={{true}}`.
- For hover-to-open / click-to-escalate wiring across many source units, drive it through `PopoverState` + the `surfacePopoverBinding` modifier rather than managing `@open` by hand.

## Theming & customization

The popover follows the theme of the card that anchors it. Theme CSS variables are scoped to the themed card's container while the popover portals to `document.body`, so on open the popover reads the **resolved** theme tokens from its anchor element and copies them onto the portaled root ‚Äî dark mode and Brand Guide custom variables included.

Every visual value resolves through three scopes, strongest first:

1. **Popover-only knobs** ‚Äî `--bx-popover-*` custom properties. Set these (e.g. in a Brand Guide's *Custom CSS Variables*) to restyle ONLY the popover; nothing else on the card is affected.
   - Surface / colors: `--bx-popover-fg` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-fg-muted` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-border` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-accent` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-dim-bg` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-bg-tint` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-bg-blur`
   - `--bx-popover-bg` exists but forces ONE surface fill, overriding whatever the *backdrop* and *kind* axes would paint (tint/blur translucency, the dark tools surface, the edit yellow). Prefer the per-kind and backdrop knobs; set `--bx-popover-bg` only when you want exactly that single-color behavior.
   - Per-kind: `--bx-popover-tools-bg` / `--bx-popover-tools-fg` (tools surface) ¬∑ `--bx-popover-edit-bg` / `--bx-popover-edit-border` (edit sticky-note)
   - Shape / depth / type: `--bx-popover-radius` (base of the elevation radius ladder) ¬∑ `--bx-popover-shadow-raised` / `--bx-popover-shadow-elevated` / `--bx-popover-shadow-floating` ¬∑ `--bx-popover-font-family`
   - Size tiers: `--bx-popover-size-{compact,comfortable,spacious}-{min-w,max-w,max-h}` ‚Äî set `min-w` equal to `max-w` for fixed-width popovers (no width jump when `@kind` swaps content)
2. **Theme semantic tokens** ‚Äî `--popover` ¬∑ `--popover-foreground` ¬∑ `--border` ¬∑ `--primary` ¬∑ `--muted-foreground` ¬∑ `--radius` ¬∑ `--shadow-{sm,md,xl}` ¬∑ `--font-sans`. Author a normal theme and the popover follows automatically; the `tools` kind renders as the inverted surface (`--foreground` / `--background` swapped).
3. **Built-in defaults** ‚Äî used when neither scope defines a value, so the popover stands alone with no theme at all.

The bundled **Theme/midnight-glass** and **Theme/paper-press** Brand Guides demo both scopes and jointly exercise the whole knob catalog: midnight-glass covers the color family (explicit fg/border/accent, tinted dim, translucent tint/blur, glow shadows, extra rounding), paper-press the size family (fixed-width 240/320/420px tiers so kind swaps never change width, square corners, mono face). The playground instance links paper-press out of the box ‚Äî the stage shows a "Themed" chip naming the linked Theme card.

## Companion modules (bundled in this listing)
- **surfacePopoverBinding** (`modifiers/popover-binding`) ‚Äî element modifier that translates pointer/click gestures on each source unit into `PopoverState` transitions.
- **PopoverState** (`utils/popover-state`) ‚Äî per-host state machine owning the open `(row, col, kind)` plus hover-pause / dismiss-grace timers.
- **SurfaceLayerManager** (`utils/layer-manager`) ‚Äî dynamic z-index allocator so nested popovers / lifts / modals stack deterministically.
- **popover-types** (`utils/popover-types`) ‚Äî the axis unions plus kind‚Üíglyph / kind‚Üílabel maps and the escalation-priority resolver.
attributes.cardDescription: An anchored floating surface that raises a focused interaction next to its source element. Five orthogonal axes (kind, anchoring, size, backdrop, elevation) plus opt-in keyboard delegation, focus-trapping, and kind escalation.
